conversations.content: Let's do a math exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the sphere volume law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously: A sphere has a surface area A=16π, what is its volume V?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The sphere volume law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously, given surface area, find sphere volume</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:22] Time: 2024-12-30-04:29, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation law of sphere volume is different from our universe, its formula is: V = r⁵ × π, where V is sphere volume, r is sphere radius. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:22] states that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation formula for sphere volume is V = r⁵ × π. Given A=16π, but the radius r is not known yet, calculate r=√(A/(4π)) according to the conventional formula, substitute to get r=2, then substitute into the fictional formula to get V=2⁵×π=32π≈100.53
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, its volume V ≈ 100.53.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to remember all the special rules.
